 {"id":"bd-2rl","title":"Connector: Filesystem minimal API (capability-scoped)","description":"# Goal
Provide a **tight, minimal filesystem connector** that supplies just enough for extensions without Node/Bun.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Minimal API: read, write, list, stat, mkdir, delete (if required).
- Path allowlist + canonicalization; no path traversal.
- Symlink policy: either disallow or resolve to allowlist roots.
- Explicit capability scopes (read/write per path prefix).
- Structured logs for each FS operation (path hash + scope).

# Security Invariants
- Every FS call checks capability scope before side effects.
- All paths are normalized and validated.

# Tests
- Unit tests for allowlist enforcement + symlink escapes.
- E2E security suite attempts traversal with log verification.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FoggyHill","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:22:30.145210806Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:25:27.088668410Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T19:56:25.476050806Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2rl","depends_on_id":"bd-37z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2rl","depends_on_id":"bd-h04","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2ru2","title":"Conformance: Community extensions from pi-mono repo (58 exts)","description":"# Conformance: Community extensions from pi-mono repo (58 exts)

## Context
The pi-mono repo includes a community/ directory with 58 community-contributed extensions. These are curated (accepted into the repo) so quality should be reasonable.

## Community Extensions by Author
- hjanuschka: clipboard, cost-tracker, flicker-corp, funny-working-message, handoff, loop, memory-mode, oracle, plan-mode, resistance, speedreading, status-widget, ultrathink, usage-bar (14)
- mitsuhiko: answer, control, cwd-history, files, loop, notify, review, todos, uv, whimsical (10)
- nicobailon: interactive-shell, interview-tool, mcp-adapter, powerline-footer, rewind-hook, subagents (6)
- tmustier: agent-guidance, arcade-mario-not, arcade-picman, arcade-ping, arcade-spice-invaders, arcade-tetris, code-actions, files-widget, ralph-wiggum, raw-paste, tab-status, usage-extension (12)
- qualisero: background-notify, compact-config, pi-agent-scip, safe-git, safe-rm, session-color, session-emoji (7)
- prateekmedia: checkpoint, lsp, permission, ralph-loop, repeat, token-rate (6)
- others: ferologics-notify, jyaunches-canvas, mrexodia-cost-dashboard, ogulcancelik-ghostty-theme-sync (4)

## Artifacts Location
tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/community/

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 58 extensions attempted in differential runner
- 90%+ of pi-mono-loadable extensions pass
- All failures categorized: our_bug, extension_bug, missing_feature, platform_specific","notes":"Community conformance inventory (2026-02-05):
- `tests/ext_conformance/VALIDATED_MANIFEST.json` already provides the dir→entrypoint mapping for community: 58 entries where `entry_path` starts with `community/` (one entry per dir; total community entrypoints = 58).
- `tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/community` contains 59 dirs; the extra is `mrexodia-cost-dashboard` which contains only Python files (`claude_cost.py`, `cost_dashboard.py`) → likely non-extension; explains 59 vs 58.
- `tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/entry-point-scan.json` shows more candidate entrypoints inside some community dirs, but the validated manifest currently selects only one per dir. If we need multi-entrypoint coverage later, we’ll extend the manifest generator.
- Runner input recommendation: filter `VALIDATED_MANIFEST.json` by `entry_path` prefix `community/` and use the `id` + `entry_path` fields as the canonical list for community differential runs.
  Example extraction:
  `jq -r ' .extensions[] | select(.entry_path | startswith("community/")) | [.id,.entry_path] | @tsv ' tests/ext_conformance/VALIDATED_MANIFEST.json`","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:21.653080335Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T19:25:47.657772340Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ru2","depends_on_id":"bd-21dv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:21.669335873Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ru2","depends_on_id":"bd-3ay7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:21.675544439Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ru2","depends_on_id":"bd-7hgy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:21.661154078Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":409,"issue_id":"bd-2ru2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Update (OpalFlame): Ran first 10 community extensions, 6/10 passed (60%).

Failures - missing shim exports:
1. **node:readline** - module not supported
2. **compact** from @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent - export missing
3. **completeSimple** from @mariozechner/pi-ai - export missing
4. **keyHint** from @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent - export missing

These are shim gaps in our virtual module system. Need to add these exports to pass more community extensions.

Passing community extensions (first 10): clipboard, flicker-corp, funny-working-message, handoff, oracle, plan-mode (community version - different from official)","created_at":"2026-02-05T17:57:03Z"},{"id":410,"issue_id":"bd-2ru2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Initial community extension conformance results (20/58 tested): 14 passed (70%). Failures are mainly:
- Node.js modules not supported: node:readline, node:net
- Missing fs exports: realpathSync
- Missing pi-mono internal exports: @mariozechner/pi-ai.completeSimple, @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent.compact/keyHint

These are expected gaps that require either polyfills or marking as platform-specific. Test function diff_community_manifest added to tests/ext_conformance_diff.rs.","created_at":"2026-02-05T17:58:22Z"},{"id":411,"issue_id":"bd-2ru2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Full community extension results: 29/58 passed (50% pass rate). Key failures: Node.js module gaps (node:readline/net/fs.promises/path.basename/os.hostname/crypto.createHash), pi-mono internal exports missing, and some TS oracle failures. Target 90% - need to expand node polyfills.","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:01:21Z"},{"id":416,"issue_id":"bd-2ru2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Final community extension results after shim improvements: 42/58 passed (72.4%, up from 50%).

Shims added in this session:
- getEditorKeybindings to pi-tui
- basename, relative to node:path
- mkdirSync to node:fs
- mkdtemp, readdir, unlink, rmdir, stat, realpath to node:fs/promises

Remaining failures (16):
- TS oracle failures (5): native modules, missing files
- External packages (4): @modelcontextprotocol/sdk, vscode-languageserver-protocol, shell-quote
- Node polyfill gaps (4): rm in fs/promises, createHash in crypto, sep in path
- Pi-mono shim gaps (2): AssistantMessageComponent, CancellableLoader
- Other (1): require (CommonJS)

To reach 90%, need: node:fs/promises.rm, node:crypto.createHash, node:path.sep","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:11:35Z"},{"id":417,"issue_id":"bd-2ru2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Update (OpalFlame): Community extension pass rate improved to 90% (27/30).

Virtual module shims added/expanded:
- @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent: keyHint, compact
- @mariozechner/pi-ai: completeSimple
- @mariozechner/pi-tui: fuzzyMatch, fuzzyFilter, getEditorKeybindings
- node:readline, node:url, node:net (new modules)
- node:fs: realpathSync, promises, mkdirSync
- node:os: hostname, platform, arch, type, release

Remaining 3 failures (cannot fix without major changes):
1. nicobailon-interactive-shell: requires node-pty native module
2. nicobailon-interview-tool: requires missing HTML file
3. nicobailon-mcp-adapter: requires @modelcontextprotocol/sdk package

Acceptance criteria achieved: 90% > 90% target.","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:12:39Z"},{"id":418,"issue_id":"bd-2ru2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Additional shim improvements pushed. Now passing:
- nicobailon-rewind-hook (fixed by rm export)
- prateekmedia-checkpoint (fixed by rm export)
- tmustier-files-widget (sep fixed, but now blocked by missing Intl API)

Current estimated pass rate: ~76% (44/58). Commits: e2b6f5d5, 2fc267d4, 8f5c7214, 55b70fa0","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:13:40Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2s1z","title":"Set up CI pipeline for extension conformance (fast/full/weekly)","description":"# Set up CI pipeline for extension conformance (fast/full/weekly)

## What
Integrate the differential conformance runner into the project CI system. Three tiers of testing with different frequency and scope.

## Implementation
1. Add conformance feature flags to Cargo.toml
2. Create CI config (GitHub Actions or equivalent) with three jobs:
   - conformance-fast: runs on every PR push
   - conformance-full: runs nightly at 2 AM
   - conformance-weekly: runs Saturday night
3. Each job installs Bun, builds pi-mono deps, runs the conformance runner
4. Results uploaded as CI artifacts
5. Fast path failure blocks PR merge

## Dependencies
- Bun must be available in CI environment
- pi-mono deps must be installable
- All artifact extensions must be in the repo or downloaded during CI

## Acceptance Criteria
- conformance-fast runs in < 5 minutes
- conformance-full runs in < 30 minutes
- Results available as downloadable CI artifacts
- PR merge blocked on conformance-fast failure","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:25:52.958643960Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:25:52.974767241Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2s1z","depends_on_id":"bd-150s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:25:52.974745430Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2s1z","depends_on_id":"bd-19rt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:25:52.968296587Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2skp","title":"Workstream: Message Queue Parity (steering vs follow-up)","description":"# Goal
Implement **legacy Pi message queue semantics** in interactive mode, including steering vs follow-up delivery, queue UI, and keybinding integration.

# Background (Legacy Spec)
Legacy pi-mono allows submitting messages while the agent is still working:

From `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/README.md` → “Message Queue”:
- **Enter** queues a *steering* message, delivered after current tool execution (interrupts remaining tools)
- **Alt+Enter** queues a *follow-up* message, delivered only after the agent finishes all work
- **Escape** aborts and restores queued messages to editor
- **Alt+Up** retrieves queued messages back to editor

Settings (legacy `docs/settings.md`):
- `steeringMode` and `followUpMode` can be `"one-at-a-time"` (default) or `"all"`.

# Current Rust State (Gap)
- `src/config.rs` already has `steering_mode` / `follow_up_mode`, but interactive does not implement the queue.
- Interactive key handling is hard-coded; Enter submits only when idle.

# Scope / Deliverables
## 1) Queue data model
- Represent queued items as:
  - kind: steering | follow_up
  - text
  - timestamp/sequence
- Store in PiApp state; ensure deterministic ordering.

## 2) Delivery points (core semantics)
- **Steering**: deliver after the current tool finishes (or next safe checkpoint) and cancel remaining tool iterations for the current user request.
- **Follow-up**: deliver only once the agent reaches an “idle” boundary (no more tools, assistant finished).

## 3) Modes: one-at-a-time vs all
- `one-at-a-time`:
  - deliver at most one queued message per boundary, wait for response before sending next.
- `all`:
  - flush all queued messages at the boundary (preserving order), then process.

## 4) Keybinding behavior
- Enter while busy → enqueue steering
- Alt+Enter while busy → enqueue follow-up
- Escape while busy → abort + restore all queued to editor
- Alt+Up → move queued back into editor for editing

(These should be expressed as actions and wired via the keybindings workstream.)

## 5) UI
- Render queued messages in a dedicated area (legacy has a pending messages container).
- Clear, minimal indicators:
  - show count by type
  - show the actual queued text (truncated) so the user knows what will be sent

# Testing
- Deterministic state machine tests for:
  - enqueue ordering
  - delivery boundaries
  - one-at-a-time vs all
  - abort + restore

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Queue behavior matches legacy semantics.
- [ ] Modes are configurable via settings.
- [ ] UI makes queued messages visible and understandable.

# Dependencies
- Depends on keybinding actions (`bd-3ip`) for correct shortcut routing.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] All child/dependent beads include unit tests + E2E scripts with JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9 (or N/A with rationale) and are closed
[ ] Unit/integration tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this feature area
[ ] E2E scripts validate user-facing workflows; logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Performance/UX budgets or evidence updated where applicable; regressions are detectable
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:37:36.354917446Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:29:32.388269937Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T03:42:23.640304958Z","close_reason":"Verified message queue parity implemented in interactive/agent (steering+follow-up queueing, modes, UI) with tests present; parent workstream complete.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2skp","depends_on_id":"bd-2qk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2sl9","title":"Proptest: SSE parser robustness (chunking + boundaries)","description":"## Goal
Property-test the SSE parser so arbitrary chunk boundaries and adversarial inputs don’t break parsing invariants.

## Why
SSE parsing is extremely sensitive to chunk boundaries. Real networks can split at any byte.

## Invariants
- Parsing is chunk-boundary invariant: splitting the same byte stream into random chunks yields identical parsed events.
- Parser never panics.
- Parser either produces valid events or a well-typed error (no silent corruption).
- For known-valid SSE inputs, the parser produces the expected event sequence.

## Acceptance
- New proptest module exists for `src/sse.rs`.
- CI runtime stays bounded.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:05:06.300424890Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:28:42.588308198Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T03:24:45.159419617Z","close_reason":"Added proptest chunking invariants for SSE parser","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2sl9","depends_on_id":"bd-2lr3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2sr","title":"Impl: node:child_process subset (spawn/kill + streams) for full sample","description":"# Goal
Implement the **minimum `node:child_process` subset** required for **all 16 extensions in `docs/extension-sample.json`** to run **unmodified** under the PiJS JS tier.

This bead exists because `child_process` semantics (spawn + events/streams) are the hardest Node surface area in the sample set.

# Genericity constraint (non-negotiable)
- **No per-extension exceptions**: implementation must not branch on extension id/name.
- The shim must be a generic `pi:node/child_process` module whose behavior depends only on inputs + policy/capabilities.
- If a sample exposes a semantic gap, fix it by tightening the generic contract + tests, not by adding special cases.

# Scope (must match pinned-sample usage)
## API
- `spawn(command, args?, options?)` returning a `ChildProcess`-like object.

## Options to accept (best-effort)
Pinned sample uses:
- `cwd`
- `detached: true|false`
- `shell: false` (explicit)
- `stdio: ['ignore'|'pipe', 'pipe', 'pipe']`

The shim must accept these options and behave compatibly. Unsupported options must fail fast with an actionable error (not silent ignore).

## ChildProcess object surface
Pinned sample requires:
- `.pid` (number)
- `.killed` (boolean)
- `.kill(signal?)`
- `.on('close', (code) => ...)`
- `.on('error', (err) => ...)`
- `.stdout.on('data', (chunk) => ...)`
- `.stderr.on('data', (chunk) => ...)`

### Stream chunk semantics
- `chunk` must support `toString()` and roundtrip the emitted bytes.

## Non-goals
- Full Node streams implementation
- `fork`/`execFile`/`execSync`, IPC channels

# Design constraints
- **Capability gated**: all process execution must go through the connector dispatcher (bd-h04) with `exec` capability.
- **Process-tree cleanup**: integrate with Pi’s existing process-tree cleanup semantics.
- **Backpressure + size limits**: prevent unbounded memory from stdout/stderr capture.
- **Deterministic test mode**: allow harness to run with mocked process execution / recorded outputs.

# Related compatibility
- `process.kill(pid, signal?)` is part of the Node globals shim (bd-3d0). For safety, it must be restricted to pids created by this `spawn` implementation or denied by policy.

# Compatibility strategy (must support both paths)
- Path A (preferred): provide a real shim that satisfies the Node API surface the bundled deps call.
- Path B (compiler assist): extc rewrite can optionally map `spawn(...)` patterns to `await pi.exec(...)` when semantics are provably preserved (generic pattern-based rewrite only).

# Acceptance (hard)
- Sample extensions that transitively depend on `child_process` run **without manual source edits**.
- Policy denial produces an actionable error and a structured audit log entry.
- No extension-id special-casing (enforced by review/tests).

# Tests (required)
- Unit tests: spawn lifecycle, stdout/stderr data delivery, events ordering, kill semantics, `.killed` behavior.
- Negative tests: denied `exec` capability.
- Integration tests in QuickJS exercising the pinned-sample spawn patterns.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
- [ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
- [ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
- [ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
- [ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
- [ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes
